channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: Vape flavour restrictions could weaken smoking quit attempts, studies suggest
item.description: Vape flavour restrictions may weaken quit attempts by making lower-risk alternatives less appealing to adult smokers. Flavours can help people move away from the taste and ritual of cigarettes, supporting a clearer break from combustible tobacco. Protecting young people is essential, but broad flavour bans risk removing one of the tools that helps smokers switch. Evidence-based policy should target youth access without undermining adult cessation. (Published on June 29, 2026byclearingtheair.eu)

item.title: CAPHRA: Philippine anti-vape commentary asks the wrong public health question
item.description: The wrong public health question is whether vaping is risk-free. The right question is whether it can reduce harm for adult smokers who would otherwise continue using combustible cigarettes. Anti-vape arguments that ignore relative risk risk leaving people with the most dangerous option still on the table. Evidence-based policy should compare realistic choices, not ideal ones. (Published on June 29, 2026byprlog.org)
